title: agrimonia pilosa subsp. japonica
intro: Agrimonia pilosa subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese agrimony, is a plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which has been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Japanese agrimony include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Japanese agrimony can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some traditional herbal remedies to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopausal women.
